scope: Directly usable Tencent Video API capability recipes refreshed through the 2026-06-21 positive-tid and anonymous real-cookie replay evidence.

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup
recipes.purpose: Use API1 to resolve one CID into cover-level metadata and its video-id family.

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup_tid537_probe_shell
recipes.purpose: Use API1 tid=537 to probe the separate success shell when you deliberately want that branch rather than canonical tid=431.
expected_highlights: video_ids_count stays 0 on the current 3-CID public matrix
notes: `537` is currently a success-shell-without-sample branch, not a field-equivalent replacement for canonical tid=431.
On the current caller-facing richness matrix it repeated the same sample-less shell shape across `mzc00200idzf2m8`, `mzc00200xxpsogl`, and `mzc002009qyd7nv`.

recipes.name: api1_single_cover_lookup_tid453_cover_shell_only
recipes.purpose: Use API1 tid=453 to probe the newly confirmed positive cover-shell-only branch when you deliberately want that branch rather than canonical tid=431.
expected_highlights: cover title stays non-empty while video_ids_count stays 0 on the current 3-CID public matrix
notes: `453` is currently a positive cover-shell-only branch, not a field-equivalent replacement for canonical tid=431.
On the current caller-facing confirmation set it repeated the same non-empty cover shell plus empty video_ids shape across `mzc00200idzf2m8`, `mzc00200xxpsogl`, and `mzc002009qyd7nv`.

recipes.name: api1_batch_cover_lookup
recipes.purpose: Use API1 to fetch multiple CID cover shells in one request.
recipes.python_demo: Exposed indirectly when the current Python demo receives a comma-separated `--cid` list, but that path is only a side path: cover-level metadata comes from the first matched CID while downstream API2 requests aggregate every returned `video_id`. Use the direct API1 URL in this recipe or the contract probe when you need a clean multi-cover contract sample.
recipes.go_demo: Exposed indirectly when the current Go demo receives a comma-separated `-cid` list, but that path is only a side path: cover-level metadata comes from the first matched CID while downstream API2 requests aggregate every returned `video_id`. Use the direct API1 URL in this recipe or the contract probe when you need a clean multi-cover contract sample.
expected_highlights: illegal CID can coexist with top-level success

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_canonical
recipes.purpose: Use API2 XML to fetch one video detail row on the canonical positive tid.
notes: `535` is the current canonical example tid for repo docs and demos.
`488`, `502`, `540`, and `541` are also confirmed positive API2 tids, but are kept as alternate recipes instead of changing the default demo inputs.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid488_alt_positive_shell
recipes.purpose: Use API2 XML on the thinner confirmed alternate positive tid 488.
expected_highlights: vid / duration / cover_list / create_time currently absent on the validated public sample
notes: `488` is now caller-facing enough to keep as a dedicated alternate-positive recipe, but it is still a thin shell rather than a canonical-detail replacement.
Current live demo and raw HTTP evidence on `z4102qfi0x4` keeps the shape at title + url with empty vid/duration/cover_list/create_time.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid502_alt_positive_shell
recipes.purpose: Use API2 XML on the richer confirmed alternate positive tid 502.
expected_highlights: retcode=0 with title + url + vid + duration present
cover_list and create_time currently present on the validated public sample
notes: `502` is currently the richest validated alternate positive shell, but it still does not reach canonical `535` richness because state/upload_src/defn/category_map stay empty.
Current live demo and raw HTTP evidence on `j4101ouc4ve` keeps the shape at title + url + vid + duration + cover_list + create_time.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid540_alt_positive_shell
recipes.purpose: Use API2 XML on one alternate confirmed positive tid.
expected_highlights: same tested success family as tid=535
notes: `540` and `541` are both confirmed positive API2 tids; this recipe pins `540` as the first alternate anchor.

Current live demo evidence on `z4102qfi0x4`, `j4101ouc4ve`, and `m4102tgsa8d` shows `540` returning a positive but structurally thin row: title/duration/url present, while state/upload_src/cover_list/category_map/defn stayed empty.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_xml_tid541_alt_positive_shell
recipes.purpose: Use API2 XML on the second confirmed alternate positive tid.
expected_highlights: same tested success family as tid=535 and tid=540
notes: `541` is a separately confirmed positive API2 tid and already has second-sample confirmation in the current summary artifact.

At least one live demo validation run on `j4101ouc4ve` returned a positive but sparse row, so `541` should be read as a valid alternate positive shell rather than a guarantee of the same field fullness as canonical `535`.

recipes.name: api2_single_detail_jsonp
recipes.purpose: Use API2 JSONP mode by setting exact lowercase `otype=json`.
notes: Wide callback families can rewrite the wrapper prefix.
The demos now expose JSONP callback override via `--api2-callback` / `-api2-callback`.
The empty callback value falls back to the default `QZOutputJson=...;` shell.
Repeated callback now behaves as first-value-wins in the tested same-day anonymous and real anonymous visitor-cookie replay collision cases.
Wrong-appkey JSONP error shells still honor callback wrapper control, and the same callback-contract conclusion was replay-stable in the tested real anonymous PC Web and Mobile H5 environments.

The currently confirmed parse-breaking representative family is wider than the original `a(` / `((` / `})();` set: `[(` / `{(` / `/*(` / `a[(` / `)(` / `](` / `}(` now also break the current summary parser, while `[` and `[[` still remain raw passthrough.
Do not generalize that into a full callback grammar rule or into `any callback containing (` is unsafe.

Do not treat nearby lower-edge tids as equivalent to canonical 535 JSONP: the anonymous 524-529 probe found 524 and 526-529 on ordinary -111005 while 525 stayed on a stable local -111013 error-family island.

recipes.name: api2_batch_lookup
recipes.purpose: Use API2 to fetch multiple detail rows in one request.
expected_highlights: mixed valid and invalid items can coexist
notes: The current Python and Go demos expose API2 dispatch chunking via `--api2-batch-size` / `-api2-batch-size`.
The demo-side batch-size guard is currently `1..32`.

recipes.name: api2_all_invalid_jsonp_batch_consumer_rule
recipes.purpose: Normalize the all-invalid JSONP batch edge case on the caller side.
expected_highlights: top-level success can still mean every row is empty-shell
if all rows are empty_shell=true, treat the batch as all-invalid

recipes.name: real_session_replay_template
recipes.purpose: Replay the canonical contract matrix under real, aged, or login-state cookie headers once raw Cookie material is available.
expected_highlights: compare real-cookie replay against the anonymous matrix
look for drift in union_platform, auth-ish keys, and current canonical contract cases
notes: The anonymous visitor-cookie capture path already has a dedicated capture script; aged/login currently rely on raw Cookie header input.
The helper tool auto-fills User-Agent / Accept / Referer / Origin, so callers only need the Cookie header text.
The replay bundle runner is the higher-level one-command entry once Cookie headers are available.

The runner now also supports `--semantics-profile full` plus `--probe-extra-callback-value / --probe-extra-callback-file` when callback pathological-tail probes should ride along with the environment replay.
In constrained local environments, `--artifact-output-dir` and `--subprocess-output-dir` let the full replay bundle land in a writable location before any manual copy-back into the repository.

recipes.name: replay_bundle_full_semantics_with_callback_tail
recipes.purpose: Run the replay bundle with full semantics plus selected callback pathological-tail probes in one pass.
recipes.environment_scope: requires explicit real/aged/login replay input; currently self-validated on the anonymous real-cookie env

canonical_parameters.env_json_or_cookie_input: provide --env-json or raw desktop/mobile Cookie headers/files

expected_highlights: summary records semantics_profile and forwarded callback values
per-environment semantics artifacts are emitted in one bundle run
writable artifact-output-dir avoids local workspace write-path quirks
notes: This recipe is about replay-tooling closure rather than a newly proven environment conclusion.
The current repository proof is a self-check under the anonymous real-cookie env, not aged/login semantic closure itself.
